bank.title: UPSC Prelims Practice Bank
bank.description: Sample bank demonstrating every supported question type.

questions.stem: Who among the following was the first Indian to become a member of the British Parliament?

explanation.summary: Dadabhai Naoroji was elected to the British House of Commons from Finsbury Central in 1892 on a Liberal Party ticket — the first Indian to do so.

perOption.A: Correct. Elected in 1892, hence the title 'Grand Old Man of India'.
perOption.B: Incorrect. Gokhale was a member of the Imperial Legislative Council in India, not the British Parliament.
perOption.C: Incorrect. Banerjee was a founder of the Indian National Association but never sat in the British Parliament.
perOption.D: Incorrect. Mehta presided over the INC in 1890 but was not a British MP.

questions.stem: Consider the following statements regarding the Non-Cooperation Movement (1920–22):

statements.text: It was launched after the passage of the Rowlatt Act and the Jallianwala Bagh massacre.
It called for the boycott of British goods, schools, and law courts.
It was withdrawn by Gandhi after the Chauri Chaura incident.
Subhas Chandra Bose led the movement in Bengal as INC President at the time.

questions.question: Which of the statements given above are correct?

explanation.summary: Statements 1, 2, 3 correctly describe the movement's trigger, programme, and termination. Statement 4 is wrong — Bose was not INC President in 1920–22 (he became President only in 1938 and 1939).

perStatement.1: Correct. The Rowlatt Act (1919) and Jallianwala Bagh (April 1919) directly catalysed the movement formally launched in August 1920.
perStatement.2: Correct. The constructive programme included boycott of British institutions and goods.
perStatement.3: Correct. Gandhi withdrew the movement in February 1922 after Chauri Chaura.
perStatement.4: Incorrect. Bose became INC President only in 1938. During 1920–22, C. R. Das and others led INC affairs.

questions.stem: Match List-I (Personality) with List-II (Newspaper/Journal) and select the correct answer:

explanation.summary: Tilak founded Kesari (Marathi); Lajpat Rai started The Punjabee; Bipin Chandra Pal edited New India; Malaviya founded The Leader (Allahabad, 1909).

perMatch.D: Malaviya — The Leader, founded at Allahabad in 1909.

questions.stem: Read the Assertion (A) and Reason (R) below and select the correct option.
questions.assertion: The Government of India Act, 1935 introduced provincial autonomy.
questions.reason: It abolished diarchy at the provincial level and introduced it at the centre.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.

explanation.summary: Both A and R are factually correct. The 1935 Act ended provincial diarchy (introduced in 1919) and gave provinces autonomy, while introducing diarchy at the centre — making R the precise mechanism by which provincial autonomy was achieved.

perOption.A: Correct. R is exactly how provincial autonomy was introduced.
perOption.B: Incorrect. R *is* the explanation, not a separate fact.

questions.stem: Arrange the following events of the Indian freedom struggle in chronological order (earliest first):

events.text: Surat Split of the Indian National Congress

explanation.summary: Partition of Bengal (1905) → Surat Split (1907) → Lucknow Pact (1916) → Rowlatt Act (1919).

perEvent.1: Partition of Bengal — 1905, by Lord Curzon.
perEvent.2: Surat Split — 1907, between Moderates and Extremists.

questions.stem: Consider the following statements about the Indus Valley Civilisation:

statements.text: The civilisation was largely urban in character.
The Great Bath was found at Mohenjo-daro.
The script has been definitively deciphered.

questions.question: How many of the above statements are correct?

explanation.summary: Statements 1 and 3 are correct. Statement 2 is wrong because the Indus civilisation was a Bronze Age culture — iron came much later. Statement 4 is wrong — the script remains undeciphered.

perStatement.1: Correct. Planned cities, drainage, granaries — the civilisation is famously urban.
perStatement.2: Incorrect. Bronze, not iron, was the metal of the period.
perStatement.3: Correct. The Great Bath is at Mohenjo-daro.
perStatement.4: Incorrect. The Indus script remains undeciphered.

questions.stem: Consider the following pairs of dynasty and capital:
questions.question: How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?

explanation.summary: Pallava capital was Kanchipuram (not Madurai — that was the Pandyas).

perPair.2: Incorrect. Pallava capital was Kanchipuram; Madurai was the Pandya capital.

questions.stem: Refer to the table below showing Five-Year Plans and their primary focus:
questions.question: Which Five-Year Plan was based on the Mahalanobis model emphasising heavy industry?

explanation.summary: The Second Plan (1956–61), drafted by P. C. Mahalanobis, prioritised heavy industries like steel, machine tools, and power.

perOption.A: Incorrect. First Plan was Harrod–Domar based, focused on agriculture.
perOption.B: Correct. Mahalanobis model — heavy industry-led growth.
perOption.C: Incorrect. Third Plan focused on self-reliance amid wars and droughts.
perOption.D: Incorrect. Fourth Plan came after the plan holiday (1966–69).

questions.stem: Identify the river labelled 'X' in the map below and the state it primarily flows through.
questions.imageAlt: Outline map of India with one river highlighted and labelled X

explanation.summary: The river labelled X originates in Chhattisgarh and flows primarily through Odisha into the Bay of Bengal — the Mahanadi.

perOption.B: Incorrect. Krishna originates in Mahabaleshwar and flows through MH/KA/TG/AP.
perOption.C: Incorrect. Godavari originates in Trimbakeshwar (MH) and flows mostly through MH/TG/AP.
perOption.D: Incorrect. Cauvery flows through Karnataka and Tamil Nadu.
